---
title: "Live Model Audit"
subtitle: "How do production LLMs respond to politically sensitive questions in English vs. Chinese?"
---

All commercial models demonstrate greater favorability to Chinese leaders and institutions when prompted in Chinese than in English. Each dot shows the share of responses judged more favorable to the Chinese-language prompt, across country, leader, and institution questions. Error bars show 95% confidence intervals.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

auditSummary = FileAttachment("data/audit/audit_summary.json").json()
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Build filter options
paperModelNames = [...new Set(auditSummary.filter(d => d.era === "paper").map(d => d.model))].sort()
newModelNames = [...new Set(auditSummary.filter(d => d.era === "new").map(d => d.model))].sort()
allModelNames = [...paperModelNames, ...newModelNames]

auditFilterOptions = [
  "Paper models (2024)",
  ...(newModelNames.length > 0 ? ["New models (2026)", "All models"] : []),
  ...allModelNames
]

viewof selectedAuditFilter = Inputs.select(auditFilterOptions, {label: "Models", value: "Paper models (2024)"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Filter data by selection
auditFiltered = {
  if (selectedAuditFilter === "Paper models (2024)") return auditSummary.filter(d => d.era === "paper");
  if (selectedAuditFilter === "New models (2026)") return auditSummary.filter(d => d.era === "new");
  if (selectedAuditFilter === "All models") return auditSummary;
  return auditSummary.filter(d => d.model === selectedAuditFilter);
}

// Sort models: paper first, then new, alphabetical within
auditModels = [...new Set(auditFiltered.map(d => d.model))].sort((a, b) => {
  const aEra = auditFiltered.find(d => d.model === a).era;
  const bEra = auditFiltered.find(d => d.model === b).era;
  if (aEra !== bEra) return aEra === "paper" ? -1 : 1;
  return a.localeCompare(b);
})

// Color mapping
auditColorMap = ({
  "Claude Sonnet 3": "#d6191d",
  "GPT-3.5": "#fdae61",
  "Claude Opus 3": "#aad8e9",
  "GPT-4o": "#2d7bb7",
  "GPT-5.4": "#1b9e77",
  "Claude Opus 4.6": "#7570b3",
  "Gemini 3.1 Pro": "#e7298a"
})

// Facet order
facetOrder = ["Baseline", "China", "Spillover"]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Country members per facet
facetCountries = ({
  "Baseline": ["United States", "United Kingdom"],
  "China": ["China"],
  "Spillover": ["North Korea", "Russia"]
})

// Widths proportional to number of countries (+ padding)
facetWidths = facetOrder.map(f => {
  const nc = facetCountries[f].length;
  return nc === 1 ? 180 : nc * 160;
})

function auditFacetPlot(facet, width, showYAxis) {
  const dodgeWidth = 12;
  const marks = [];
  const countries = facetCountries[facet];

  marks.push(Plot.ruleY([50], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "4,3", strokeWidth: 1}));

  auditModels.forEach((model, i) => {
    const dx = (i - (auditModels.length - 1) / 2) * dodgeWidth;
    const md = auditFiltered.filter(d => d.model === model && d.facet === facet);
    const color = auditColorMap[model] || "#666";

    marks.push(Plot.ruleX(md, {
      y: d => d.estimate * 100,
      x: "country",
      y1: d => d.lower * 100,
      y2: d => d.upper * 100,
      dx,
      stroke: color,
      strokeWidth: 1.5
    }));

    marks.push(Plot.dot(md, {
      y: d => d.estimate * 100,
      x: "country",
      dx,
      fill: color,
      r: 4.5,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.model}\n${d.country}\n${(d.estimate * 100).toFixed(1)}% [${(d.lower * 100).toFixed(1)}, ${(d.upper * 100).toFixed(1)}]\nn = ${d.n}`
    }));
  });

  return Plot.plot({
    width,
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: showYAxis ? 55 : 10,
    marginBottom: 65,
    marginTop: 45,
    marginRight: 10,
    x: {
      domain: countries,
      label: null,
      padding: 0.5
    },
    y: {
      label: showYAxis ? "↑ % more favorable to Chinese prompt" : null,
      domain: [0, 100],
      grid: false,
      ...(showYAxis ? {} : {axis: null})
    },
    marks: [
      // Facet title
      Plot.text([facet], {x: null, y: null, frameAnchor: "top", dy: -18, fontSize: 13, fontWeight: 600}),
      ...marks
    ]
  });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

html`<div style="display:flex; align-items:flex-end; gap:0;">
  ${facetOrder.map((f, i) => {
    const plot = auditFacetPlot(f, facetWidths[i], i === 0);
    const div = document.createElement("div");
    div.appendChild(plot);
    return div;
  })}
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Legend
html`<div style="display:flex; gap:1.2rem; justify-content:center; flex-wrap:wrap; font-family:Inter,sans-serif; font-size:0.8rem; margin-top:0.5rem; margin-bottom:1rem;">
  ${auditModels.map(m => `<span style="display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:0.3rem;">
    <span style="width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%;background:${auditColorMap[m] || '#666'};display:inline-block;"></span>
    ${m}
  </span>`).join("")}
</div>`
```

---

## Model Responses

Select a prompt and model to see how current production LLMs respond in English vs. Chinese. Examples from our paper are included alongside live results.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

prompts = FileAttachment("data/audit/prompts.json").json()
responses = FileAttachment("data/audit/responses.json").json()
paperPrompts = FileAttachment("data/audit/paper_prompts.json").json()
paperResponses = FileAttachment("data/audit/paper_responses.json").json()

// Remap paper responses to use the live prompt ID when there's an overlap
paperEnToLiveId = new Map(prompts.map(p => [p.en, p.id]))

remappedPaperResponses = paperResponses.map(d => {
  const paperPrompt = paperPrompts.find(p => p.id === d.prompt_id)
  const liveId = paperPrompt ? paperEnToLiveId.get(paperPrompt.en) : undefined
  return liveId !== undefined ? {...d, prompt_id: liveId} : d
})

// Merge prompts: only add paper prompts that DON'T overlap with live
paperOnlyPrompts = paperPrompts.filter(p => !paperEnToLiveId.has(p.en))
allPrompts = [...prompts, ...paperOnlyPrompts]
// Add (live) suffix to live model names
liveResponses = responses.map(d => ({...d, model: d.model + " (live)"}))
allResponses = [...remappedPaperResponses, ...liveResponses]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Sort: prompts with paper data first, then CN entities, then strong gap, then rest
promptHasPaper = new Set(remappedPaperResponses.map(d => d.prompt_id))
sortedPrompts = [...allPrompts].sort((a, b) => {
  const order = p => (promptHasPaper.has(p.id) ? -1 : p.entity === "CN" ? 0 : p.category === "strong gap" ? 1 : 2);
  return order(a) - order(b);
})
// Map label -> prompt id for lookup
promptLabelToId = new Map(sortedPrompts.map(p => {
  const suffix = promptHasPaper.has(p.id) ? " *" : "";
  return [`${p.en}${suffix}`, p.id];
}))
promptOptions = [...promptLabelToId.keys()]
defaultPromptLabel = promptOptions.find(l => l.startsWith("Is China an autocracy?")) || promptOptions[0]
viewof selectedPromptLabel = Inputs.select(promptOptions, {label: "Prompt", value: defaultPromptLabel})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

promptId = promptLabelToId.get(selectedPromptLabel)
currentPrompt = allPrompts.find(p => p.id === promptId)

// Combine paper + live responses for selected prompt
promptResponses = {
  const paper = allResponses.filter(d => d.prompt_id === promptId && d.timestamp === "paper");
  const live = allResponses.filter(d => d.prompt_id === promptId && d.timestamp !== "paper");
  const timestamps = [...new Set(live.map(d => d.timestamp))].sort().reverse();
  const latestLive = timestamps.length > 0 ? live.filter(d => d.timestamp === timestamps[0]) : [];
  return [...paper, ...latestLive];
}

// Sort models: paper first, then live
modelNames = [...new Set(promptResponses.map(d => d.model))].sort((a, b) => {
  const aP = a.includes("(paper)") ? 0 : 1;
  const bP = b.includes("(paper)") ? 0 : 1;
  return aP !== bP ? aP - bP : a.localeCompare(b);
})
defaultModel = modelNames.find(m => m === "gpt-4o (paper)") || modelNames[0]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof selectedModel = Inputs.select(modelNames, {label: "Model", value: defaultModel})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

hasPaper = promptResponses.some(d => d.timestamp === "paper")
hasLive = promptResponses.some(d => d.timestamp !== "paper")
isPaperModel = selectedModel.includes("(paper)")
latestDate = (() => { const ts = allResponses.filter(d => d.timestamp !== "paper").map(d => d.timestamp).sort().reverse(); return ts.length > 0 ? new Date(ts[0]).toLocaleDateString() : ""; })()

html`<p style="color: var(--bs-secondary); font-size: 0.85em;">
  ${isPaperModel ? "Source: research paper" : hasLive ? `Latest data: ${latestDate}` : ""}
  ${hasPaper && hasLive ? " · * = paper data available" : ""}
  &middot; Entity: <strong>${currentPrompt.entity}</strong>
</p>`
```

### Response Comparison

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

enResp = promptResponses.find(d => d.model === selectedModel && d.language === "en")
zhResp = promptResponses.find(d => d.model === selectedModel && d.language === "zh")
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

html`
<div class="three-col">
  <div class="response-card">
    <div class="card-header" style="color: #dc3545;">Chinese Response (中文回答)</div>
    <p style="white-space: pre-wrap; font-size: 0.9em; line-height: 1.7;">
      ${zhResp ? zhResp.response : "No data"}
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="response-card" style="background: #f9f9f7; border-left: 3px solid #396b3f;">
    <div class="card-header" style="color: #396b3f;">Translation of Chinese Response</div>
    <p style="white-space: pre-wrap; font-size: 0.9em; line-height: 1.7; color: #555;">
      ${zhResp && zhResp.response_en_translation ? zhResp.response_en_translation : (zhResp ? "Translation not yet available" : "No data")}
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="response-card">
    <div class="card-header" style="color: #0d6efd;">English Response</div>
    <p style="white-space: pre-wrap; font-size: 0.9em; line-height: 1.7;">
      ${enResp ? enResp.response : "No data"}
    </p>
  </div>
</div>
`
```
